title: oenothera polymorpha var. stricta
intro: Oenothera polymorpha var. stricta, also known as the "fringed penstemon" or "california evening primrose," is a perennial plant species belonging to the evening primrose family (Onagraceae) and is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Oenothera polymorpha var. stricta include its potential to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. This plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, and its roots and flowers can be used to make salves or creams for topical application.
